site.title: NazivIER – IER researcher-title calculator
site.description: Internal tool of the Institute for Economic Research (IER, Ljubljana) for automatic calculation of researcher titles under the new rulebook, powered by SICRIS.
site.ogDescription: Automatic title-eligibility check under the IER researcher-titles rulebook (draft 5 Jun 2026, v2.2). Bibliography and clean citations from SICRIS (WoS / Scopus).

header.eyebrow: Institute for Economic Research · internal tool
header.title: NazivIER – researcher-title calculator
header.subtitle1: Automatic eligibility check against the new
header.subtitle2: (draft 5 Jun 2026, v2.2), Annexes 2 and 3. Bibliography and clean citations from SICRIS (WoS or Scopus). Authorship factor entered manually per publication.

picker.pasteUrl: or 2. Paste a SICRIS URL / enter an ID
picker.invalidInput: Enter a valid SICRIS ID (e.g. 33182) or profile URL.
picker.rosterError: Could not load the list ({msg}). Use the field on the right to enter a SICRIS ID manually.

loading: Fetching bibliography and clean citations from SICRIS, and projects from ier.si … (a few seconds on first request, cached after).

bibUnavailable.label: Bibliography temporarily unavailable.
bibUnavailable.body: The SICRIS/COBISS server is not responding for {name} right now (overloaded or briefly down). Please try again in a few moments.

metadata.title: Data not exposed by public sources
metadata.subtitle: Bibliography, clean citations and projects are fetched automatically from SICRIS and ier.si. Important: the authorship factor (first/lead/corresponding, equal, other) must be confirmed manually per publication — without it the computed equivalents are only a rough estimate.

fields.externalProjectsValueEur: Pogoj 2: cumulative value of non-ARIS projects led by candidate (EUR)
fields.externalProjectsValueEurHelp: Sum of total funding for projects outside ARIS where the candidate acted as project leader. Per-title thresholds: 20,000 / 150,000 / 300,000 EUR (sodelavec / višji / svetnik).
fields.leadershipValueEur: Pogoj 3 (a–d): cumulative value of led projects or work packages (EUR)
fields.leadershipValueEurHelp: Sum of total funding (for the Institute + other partners) on projects or work packages where the candidate had a leading role at project level. Per-title thresholds: 50,000 / 250,000 / 500,000 EUR.
fields.leadershipYears: Pogoj 3 (e): years in an executive function
fields.leadershipYearsHelp: Only the five rulebook-enumerated functions count: director, chair of the management board, chair of the scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group (at the Institute or an equivalent institution). Project leadership years do NOT belong here.
fields.leadershipYearsTooltip: At least 1 / 2 / 3 years holding an executive function: director, chair of management board, chair of scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group.
fields.weight10Count: Weight-1.0 achievements without typology (completed PhD mentorships, Zois/international awards)
fields.weight05Count: Weight-0.5 achievements without typology (journal editorial board, completed leadership of research project/programme)
fields.reelectionHelp: Tick if this is a re-election to the same title. Rulebook v2.2 (Article 22, paragraph 2) requires TWO things: (1) since the previous election the candidate must meet at least half of every first-election threshold – auto-applied via halved thresholds below. (2) The candidate must show progress equivalent to ≥20 % of the gap in at least two quantitative conditions between the current and next-higher title – this cannot be checked automatically (would require a previous-application snapshot); the commission must confirm it manually.
fields.additionalDepositsHelp: We detected {deposited} of {total} post-2024 publications as open access ({missing} missing). If you have already deposited or will deposit additional works in the institutional / thematic repository before the application is reviewed, enter the count here. The evaluator recomputes the ratio against the 100 % threshold in Article 11(6). This never bypasses the rule, it records the realistic state.
fields.ierLeadershipLabel: Project leadership from ier.si (audit only — does NOT auto-fill Pogoj 3)
fields.ierLeadershipHelp: From the IER website we detected the candidate leads {count} projects ({external} outside pure ARIS funding), spanning about {years} years. Projects for audit:
fields.ierLeadershipDisclaimer: Important: project-leadership years do NOT by themselves satisfy Pogoj 3 path (e). The rulebook requires years holding an executive function (director, chair of management board, chair of scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group). These projects are surfaced solely as audit evidence and can support manually entered EUR values for Pogoj 3 (a–d) or Pogoj 2 (value of non-ARIS projects).
fields.authorshipPerPubHelp: Rulebook v2.2 requires the candidate to declare the authorship type per publication: first / lead / corresponding (factor 1.0), equal (0.7), or other co-authorship (0.3). The SICRIS bibliography does not expose this, so every entry defaults to 0.7. Tag your actual role – the evaluator recomputes the equivalents. Note: a publication tagged »equal« cannot also claim »first/corresponding« (Notes on criteria, point 4).

breakdownGroups.showTitles: {count, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}} in group
breakdownGroups.openHint: Click to see {count, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}}

osDemo.title: Demo mode for the Open-Science requirement (Article 11, paragraph 6):
osDemo.body: Article 11(6) is new and in this demo it is treated as fulfilled for every researcher because the rulebook is still a draft (proposal of 26 May 2026). The true open-access status is still computed and displayed on each title card and in the summary strip so candidates can see how many deposits separate them from 100 % coverage.

duplicates.title: Possible duplicates in the bibliography:
duplicates.summary: {groups, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}} appear more than once ({records} records, ~{eq} equivalents combined). The algorithm still counts each record separately – this is a reviewer hint, not a score adjustment.
duplicates.disclaimer: The rulebook (Annex 3) sums every COBISS record at its own weight, so this list is informational only. During qualitative review (Article 12) the commission can decide whether duplicate records represent genuinely distinct outputs (conference paper + journal version, SL + EN translation, preprint + final) or a cataloguing issue – COBISS cataloguing hygiene is the librarian's responsibility, not the calculator's.

diagnostics.algoTooltip: Algorithm / rulebook version driving the calculation
diagnostics.detailsSummary: Technical details for a bug report
diagnostics.detailsHelp: Copy the block below and include it in a bug report. It contains the version, researcher ID, timestamp and error details. No personal data.

versionWatcher.message: A new version is available ({version}). Refresh for the latest calculation.

evalCard.contributionsSummary: {eq} equivalents from {count, plural, one {# publication} other {# publications}}
evalCard.reelectionBadge: Re-election (thresholds halved) – commission must manually confirm ≥20 % progress in ≥2 conditions
evalCard.reelectionTooltip: Article 22(2) of rulebook v2.2: on re-election the candidate must meet at least the halved thresholds (auto) AND show ≥20 % progress in at least 2 quantitative conditions between current and next-higher title (commission judgment).
evalCard.eligibleWithoutPogoj1Note: Eligible despite an unmet Pogoj 1 (published output) – the rulebook allows meeting 2 of 3 conditions. Check that this is intended.
evalCard.selfReportedNote: Eligibility partly rests on manually entered, unverified values (Pogoj 2: value of non-ARIS projects; Pogoj 3: leadership value or years).

equivalent.head: Achievement equivalent
equivalent.body: = achievement weight × authorship factor (Rulebook, Annex 3, Notes on criteria).

weights.head: Weights (Rulebook v2.2)
weights.body: follow Annex 3: 2.0 for granted patents with full examination (2.24); 1.5 for 1.01/1.02 in Q1 (1A1 in COBISS); 1.0 for 1.01/1.02 in Q2 (1A2 in COBISS), 1.16, 2.01; 0.7 for other peer-reviewed articles; 0.5 for invited abstracts and professional monographs; 0.3 for professional articles and reports; 0.1 for popular contributions.

q12.head: Q1/Q2 status
q12.body: is determined primarily via an embedded SCImago JR snapshot keyed by journal ISSN-L (economics, finance and adjacent journals). When the ISSN is not in the snapshot, we fall back to a heuristic: 1.01/1.02 publications are ranked by impact, the top quartile is tagged Q1, the next Q2.

authorship.head: Authorship factor
authorship.body: : SICRIS bibliography export does not expose the corresponding author, so default is 0.7 (equal). Options are first/lead/corresponding (1.0), equal (0.7) and other co-authorship (0.3). The per-publication role can be overridden in the panel above.

citations.head: Clean citations
citations.body: : the official number for the rulebook check is SICRIS WoS (»čisti citati po WoS« – what ARIS evaluators see – fetched with a two-step call to bib.cobiss.net/biblioweb/cit/…). SICRIS Scopus is used as a fallback. Self-citations are excluded.

leadership.head: Leadership capability (Pogoj 3)
leadership.body: : the rulebook v2.2 (Annex 3) offers two alternative paths. (a–d) Cumulative value of completed led projects or work packages ≥ 50,000 / 250,000 / 500,000 EUR (by career stage) – entered manually, because ier.si does not publish EUR. (e) At least 1 / 2 / 3 years holding an executive function: director, chair of the management board, chair of the scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group. Years of project leadership (from ier.si/projekti) do NOT qualify for path (e); they are shown as an audit list under the metadata panel.

openScience.head: Open Science (Article 11, paragraph 6)
openScience.body: : the Open-Science requirement is new in the IER rulebook (draft of 5 Jun 2026, Article 11(6)–(8)). It explicitly transposes the Slovenian Ordinance on the conduct of scientific-research work (Official Gazette RS, 59/23). It applies only to scientific publications subject to evaluation (1.01, 1.02, 1.03, 1.06, 1.08, 1.16, 1.26) created from 2024 onward; interviews, reviews, professional and popular pieces are excluded. In this demo we therefore treat it as fulfilled for every researcher – while the rulebook is still a proposal we do not want to block eligibility. The true OA status is still computed and displayed so candidates see how far they are from 100 % coverage.

earlyPromotion.head: Early promotion (Article 14, paragraph 5)
earlyPromotion.body: : we flag candidates who exceed the minimum thresholds by ≥ 50 % AND meet the years-in-research-sector floor (≥ 10 yrs for stage III, ≥ 15 yrs for stage IV). Shown as a badge on each title card.

reelection.head: Re-election (Article 22, paragraph 2)
reelection.body: : when you tick »re-election« in the meta panel, every numeric threshold is halved – rulebook v2.2 requires at least half of the first-election values. The second requirement (≥20 % progress in ≥2 quantitative conditions between current and next-higher title) remains a commission judgment because the calculator has no snapshot of the previous application.

footer: Methodology per the rulebook of 5 Jun 2026 (v2.2), Annex 3. Typology per the COBISS typology of documents/works. Clean citations from SICRIS WoS (official), fallback SICRIS Scopus. Authorship factor defaults to 0.7 (equal) – set to 1.0 per publication for first / lead / corresponding author.

exportToolbar.emailHint: Opens your default mail client with all your inputs. Send to yourself or to Kaja as a portable backup.
exportToolbar.emailIntro: Attached is a NazivIER backup for {name}. Paste the JSON into the »Restore from text« field to recover inputs in the tool.
exportToolbar.restoreHint: Paste a previously saved JSON (e.g. from an email backup) to restore your inputs.
exportToolbar.restoreIntro: Paste the JSON you received in a »NazivIER backup …« email. The tool will restore every manual input for this researcher.
exportToolbar.restorePlaceholder: Paste JSON here (with or without ``` fences) …
exportToolbar.restoreInvalid: Could not parse the content as a valid NazivIER JSON backup.
exportToolbar.restoreMismatch: Backup belongs to a different researcher (SICRIS {have}); currently loaded: {want}. Switch to the matching researcher first.
exportToolbar.resetHint: Removes only your manual inputs for this researcher from this browser. SICRIS data stays.
exportToolbar.resetConfirm: Really clear all manual inputs for this researcher (locally and in the shared institute store)? This cannot be undone.
exportToolbar.autosaveTooltip: Inputs are auto-saved to your browser (localStorage). Reopen on the same device and researcher and they come back.
exportToolbar.explainer: Inputs are auto-saved to your browser (localStorage, keyed by SICRIS ID). For cross-device transfer use »Email backup« plus »Restore from text«. For the final version use »Print / save as PDF« or »Copy summary«.
exportToolbar.sharedExplainer: Inputs are also saved to the shared institute store so the administrator (Kaja) sees the real state. The record is visible and editable to everyone at the institute.
exportToolbar.sharedSavedBy: Shared institute store · last edited by {who} · {when}

kajaCalibration.lockTooltip: Simulation view (internal) – click to enter password
kajaCalibration.modalIntro: Enter the password to display alternative calculations alongside the rulebook (informational only, does not affect pass/fail).
kajaCalibration.simSpec: Move the sliders and toggles to see how the Rulebook and the algorithm react under different assumptions. Every change updates Σ equivalents, citations, leadership values and per-title pass/fail. The rulebook result above remains untouched.
kajaCalibration.simHint: Alternative calculation under the chosen assumptions.
kajaCalibration.deltaAbove: Simulation Σ is above the rulebook Σ.
kajaCalibration.deltaBelow: Simulation Σ is below the rulebook Σ.
kajaCalibration.sec0Subtitle: Weights by journal quartile; default per Rulebook v2.2
kajaCalibration.sec0Method: Default weights per Rulebook v2.2 (Annex 3): 1.01/1.02 in Q1 = 1.5, in Q2 = 1.0, outside Q1/Q2 = 0.7; 1.03 = 0.7. The sliders change these weights in the simulation only – the canonical calculation (weights.ts / evaluate.ts) stays untouched for every researcher. The »Kaja proposal« preset sets Q1 = Q2 = 1.5, outside = 1.0 and 1.03 = 1.0.
kajaCalibration.q12Label: Typology 1.01 and 1.02 without a Q1/Q2 tag
kajaCalibration.q12Hint: Count as Q1/Q2 (weight 1.0) instead of the default 0.7
kajaCalibration.sec1Method: Σ = Σ(weight × authorship factor) over all publications with a valid typology (Annex 3) + extra achievements (awards, mentorships, editorships). Weights per Rulebook 26 May 2026: 1.01/1.02 in Q1/Q2 = 1.0; otherwise 0.7. 1.16/2.01/2.24 = 1.0. 1.03/1.06/1.07 = 0.7. Etc.
kajaCalibration.sec2Title: 2. Pogoj 2 – citations OR value of led non-ARIS projects
kajaCalibration.sec2Subtitle: Clean-citations source and project value in EUR
kajaCalibration.externalProjectsValueEurLabel: Value of non-ARIS projects led by candidate (EUR)
kajaCalibration.sec2Method: Rulebook v2.2: Pogoj 2 passes if at least one of the two holds – (a) Σ clean citations ≥ threshold for the chosen title, or (b) cumulative value of non-ARIS projects led by the candidate ≥ EUR threshold (scientific / professional-research: 20k/150k/300k EUR). Default source is SICRIS WoS (what an ARIS reviewer sees).
kajaCalibration.sec3Subtitle: Cumulative EUR value and years in a leadership function
kajaCalibration.leadershipValueEurLabel: Cumulative value of led projects or work packages (EUR)
kajaCalibration.leadershipYearsLabel: Years in a leadership function (director, board chair, science-council chair, programme/infrastructure group head)
kajaCalibration.sec3Method: Rulebook v2.2: two alternative paths – (a–d) cumulative value of completed led projects or work packages ≥ 50k / 250k / 500k EUR (by stage), or (e) years served in the exhaustively listed leadership functions: institute director, chair of the management board, chair of the science council, head of the programme group, head of the infrastructure group.
kajaCalibration.sec4Method: Rulebook (Art. 11(6)): all evaluated publications created after Decree 59/23 (year ≥ 2024, typology 1.x) must be 100 % open access. The carve-out (»except where publisher restrictions make it impossible«) is included: publications manually flagged »Restricted – not possible« count as satisfied.
kajaCalibration.sec5Subtitle: Multiply every min* value across all titles
kajaCalibration.sec5Method: Multiplies every threshold (minEquivalents, minCitations, minExternalProjectsValueEur, minLeadershipValueEur, minLeadershipYears) by the chosen value. 0.5 = halved thresholds (as in re-election per Art. 22(2)); 2.0 = doubled; 1.5 ≈ early-promotion check per Art. 14(5).
kajaCalibration.perTitleSpec: All thresholds apply the chosen multiplier. ✓ means the condition is met, ✗ means it isn't. The overall verdict requires education, enough standards and Open Science.
kajaCalibration.reminderNotPassFail: All results in this panel are simulation only – the rulebook pass/fail in the main evaluation remains untouched.
kajaCalibration.limit_weights: Weights apply only in this simulation panel. If the committee adopts the proposal, the new weights ship as a new algorithm version (ALGO_VERSION) for every researcher uniformly – never per individual. The quartile is currently static (no publication year); see the 1.01/1.02 limit.
kajaCalibration.limit_authorship: The authorship factor comes from the role manually set on each publication (default equal 0.7); before trusting the simulation, review the roles in the Metadata section.
kajaCalibration.limit_senior: »Lead« author is not separated from »first/corresponding« in the SICRIS feed; if the researcher is a senior author (last in the list), mark the publication as »first/lead/corresponding« manually.
kajaCalibration.limit_corresponding: Corresponding author is not auto-detected from SICRIS – mark manually on publications where the researcher is the corresponding author.
kajaCalibration.limit_q12: 1.01/1.02 without a Q1/Q2 tag count at weight 0.7. The SCImago quartile is detected only for ~60 economics journals in the local seed table; for the rest trust the SICRIS 1A1/1A2 marker.
kajaCalibration.limit_selfcite: SICRIS WoS and Scopus figures from the bibliography scrape are already »clean citations« (self-citations excluded).
kajaCalibration.limit_projectValue: The EUR value of non-ARIS projects is entered manually – ier.si does not publish per-researcher EUR values for led projects.
kajaCalibration.limit_leadershipValue: Cumulative EUR value of led projects / work packages is entered manually – the ier.si project list does not expose per-researcher EUR shares.
kajaCalibration.limit_leadershipYears: »Years in a leadership function« applies strictly to the five listed functions in the Rulebook; years spent leading individual research projects do not count (v2.7.2 correction).
kajaCalibration.limit_osDetection: Open-access detection relies on automatic data sources; if a publication does not match it is treated as »closed« until the user marks the exemption.
kajaCalibration.limit_osExemption: The rulebook carve-out (»publisher restrictions«) requires a manual »restricted-not-possible« tag – it is not auto-detected.
kajaCalibration.limit_threshold: The multiplier affects every numeric threshold uniformly; it does not affect the education level (minEducation) or standardsRequired.
